title: Print Your Name
description: Write a program that prints your name to the screen using `print()`.
hints: Use the print() function with your name in quotes

testCases.description: Must output at least 1 line

title: Print Multiple Lines
description: Print your name, your age, and your favorite color, each on a separate line, using three separate `print` statements.
hints: Use three separate print() calls, one for each item

testCases.description: Must output exactly 3 lines

title: Variables
description: Create a variable called `name` and assign it your name. Then print `My name is` followed by the variable.

testCases.description: Output must contain 'My name is'

title: Simple Arithmetic
description: Create two variables `a = 5` and `b = 3`. Print the result of `a + b`, `a - b`, `a * b`, and `a / b` each on separate lines.

title: User Input
description: Ask the user for their name using `input()`, store it in a variable, and then print a greeting like `Hello, [name]!` using an f-string.
hints: Use input('Enter your name: ') to get user input

title: String Concatenation
description: Ask for first name and last name separately using `input()`, then print the full name by concatenating them with a space in between.
hints: Don't forget the space: first + ' ' + last

title: Type Conversion
description: Ask the user for a number (as a string via `input()`), convert it to an integer using `int()`, multiply it by 2, and print the result.
hints: Use int() to convert string to integer

title: Working with Strings
description: Create a variable `sentence = "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"`. Print the length of the sentence using `len()`. Then print the first 10 characters using slicing.

title: Lists Introduction
description: Create a list `fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]`. Print the list, then print the first element and the last element.
hints: Use fruits[0] for first, fruits[-1] for last

title: List Operations
description: Start with `fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]`. Append "orange" to the list, then remove "banana". Print the final list.

title: Even or Odd
description: Ask the user for a number using `input()` and print whether it is `Even` or `Odd` using an `if` statement and the modulo operator `%`.
hints: Use num % 2 == 0 to check if even

title: Largest of Three
description: Ask for three numbers using `input()` and print the largest one. Do NOT use the built-in `max()` function.
hints: Use if/elif/else to compare all three

title: For Loop - Sum
description: Use a `for` loop with `range()` to calculate the sum of all numbers from 1 to 100. Print the result.

title: While Loop - Countdown
description: Use a `while` loop to print numbers from 10 down to 1, then print "Blast off!" at the end.
hints: Start with n = 10, loop while n >= 1, decrement each iteration

title: Loop through a List
description: Given `fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]`, use a `for` loop to print each fruit on a separate line.

title: List Sum
description: Given `numbers = [4, 7, 2, 9, 5]`, write a `for` loop to calculate the sum. Do NOT use the built-in `sum()` function. Print the result.
hints: Create a total = 0 variable, add each number in the loop

title: Find Maximum in List
description: Using a loop, find the largest number in `[3, 7, 2, 9, 5]` without using `max()`. Print the result.
hints: Track the largest number seen so far in a variable

title: Break - Find First Even
description: Write a loop over `[3, 7, 8, 2, 9]` that uses `break` to stop when it finds the first even number. Print that number.

title: Continue - Skip Multiples of 3
description: Print numbers from 1 to 20, but skip any number divisible by 3. Use `continue` to skip.

title: Nested Loop - Multiplication Table
description: Print the multiplication table for numbers 1 through 5 in a grid format using nested loops. Each row should have numbers separated by spaces.
hints: Use print(i*j, end=' ') and print() for newline

title: Simple Function
description: Write a function called `greet()` that prints "Hello, welcome!" when called. Then call the function.

title: Function with Parameter
description: Write a function `greet_person(name)` that prints "Hello, [name]!". Call it with any name you like.

testCases.description: Output must contain 'Hello,'

title: Return Value
description: Write a function `add(a, b)` that returns the sum of a and b. Call it with `add(5, 3)` and print the result.

title: Function with Default Parameter
description: Write a function `power(base, exponent=2)` that returns base raised to exponent. Print `power(3)` and `power(2, 3)`.
hints: def power(base, exponent=2): return base ** exponent

title: Multiple Return Values
description: Write a function `get_min_max(numbers)` that returns both the minimum and maximum values from a list as a tuple. Test with `[3, 1, 9, 4, 7]`.

title: Docstrings
description: Write a function `get_min_max` with a docstring that says "Returns the minimum and maximum values from a list." Then print the docstring using `print(get_min_max.__doc__)`.
hints: Use triple quotes inside the function as first line

testCases.expectedOutput: Returns the minimum and maximum values from a list.

title: Scope
description: Create a variable `x = 10` outside a function. Write a function that modifies x to 20 using `global`. Print x before and after calling the function.
hints: Use the global keyword inside the function

title: Using Functions from Previous Problems
description: Write a function `calculate_average(numbers)` that returns the average. Use it on `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]` and print the result.
hints: Sum the numbers, divide by len()

title: Recursion - Factorial
description: Write a recursive function `factorial(n)` that returns n!. Print `factorial(5)` (should be 120).

title: Recursion - Fibonacci
description: Write a recursive function `fibonacci(n)` that returns the nth Fibonacci number. Print `fibonacci(5)` (should be 5).

title: String Slicing
description: Given `s = "Hello, World!"`, use string slicing to extract and print just the word "World".

title: String Methods
description: Given `sentence = "the quick brown fox"`, print it in lowercase, then uppercase, then title case, each on a separate line.

testCases.expectedOutput:
the quick brown fox
THE QUICK BROWN FOX
The Quick Brown Fox

title: Split and Join
description: Split the sentence "The quick brown fox" into words, then join them back with hyphens. Print the result.

title: Character Count
description: Write a function `count_char(text, char)` that returns how many times `char` appears in `text`. Test with count_char("hello world", "l") and print the result.
hints: Loop through each character and count matches

title: Palindrome Checker
description: Write a function `is_palindrome(s)` that returns True if s reads the same forwards and backwards (case insensitive). Test with "racecar".

title: List Comprehension - Squares
description: Use a list comprehension to create a list of squares of numbers from 1 to 10. Print the list.

title: List Comprehension - Even Numbers
description: From numbers 1 to 10, use a list comprehension with a condition to create a list of even numbers only. Print it.
hints: Add an if condition: [x for x in range(1,11) if x % 2 == 0]

title: Enumerate
description: Given `fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]`, use `enumerate` to print each fruit with its index in format "0: apple".
hints: for i, fruit in enumerate(fruits): print(f'{i}: {fruit}')

title: Zip
description: Use `zip` to pair `names = ["Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"]` and `scores = [85, 92, 78]`, printing "Alice scored 85" etc.
hints: for name, score in zip(names, scores):

testCases.expectedOutput:
Alice scored 85
Bob scored 92
Charlie scored 78

title: List of Tuples Sorting
description: Given `pairs = [("Alice", 85), ("Bob", 92), ("Charlie", 78)]`, sort by score in descending order using `sorted()` with a `key`. Print the sorted list.

title: Creating a Dictionary
description: Create a dictionary `student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 20, "grade": "A"}`. Print it.
hints: Use curly braces {} with key: value pairs

title: Accessing Dictionary Values
description: Given `student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 20, "grade": "A"}`, print the name and age on separate lines.

title: Adding and Updating
description: Given `student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 20, "grade": "A"}`, add "major": "Computer Science" and update grade to "A+". Print the final dictionary.

title: Looping through a Dictionary
description: Given `student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 20, "grade": "A"}`, loop through and print each "key: value" on separate lines.
hints: for key, value in student.items(): print(f'{key}: {value}')

title: Word Frequency Counter
description: Write a function `word_count(sentence)` that returns a dict of word frequencies. Test with "the cat and the dog".
hints: Split sentence into words, loop and count using a dict

title: Simulated File Reading
description: Given a multi-line string `text = """Hello World\nPython is great\nI love coding"""`, loop through the lines and print each one.

testCases.expectedOutput:
Hello World
Python is great
I love coding

title: Simulated File Writing
description: Simulate file operations: create a list called `content`, append "Hello World" and "New line added", then print each line.
hints: Use a list as a buffer, append lines, then print

title: Counting Lines
description: Given a multi-line string with 5 lines, count and print the number of lines.
hints: Split by newline and use len()

title: Word Frequency from Text
description: Given `text = "the cat and the dog and the fish"`, count word frequencies and print the dictionary.
hints: Split text into words, use a dict to count

title: Most Common Word
description: Given `freq = {"the": 3, "cat": 1, "and": 2, "dog": 1}`, find and print the word with the highest count.
hints: Loop through items, track the one with highest value

title: Clean Text Function
description: Write `clean_text(text)` that converts to lowercase and removes punctuation. Test with a sample sentence.
hints: Think about how string cleaning works
Break the problem into smaller steps

testCases.description: Code must compile and produce output

title: Math Sum Drill 101
description: Read an integer n and print the sum of numbers from 1 to n.
hints: Use a loop or formula n*(n+1)//2.

title: AP nth Term 102
description: Input a, d, n and print the nth term of an arithmetic progression.

title: GCD Pair 103
description: Read two integers and print their greatest common divisor (GCD).
hints: Use repeated modulo until remainder is 0.
